column1: As an AI image tagging expert, please provide precise tags for these images to enhance CLIP model's understanding of the content. Employ succinct keywords or phrases, steering clear of elaborate sentences and extraneous conjunctions. Prioritize the tags by relevance. Your tags should capture key elements such as the main subject, setting, artistic style, composition, image quality, color tone, filter, and camera specifications, and any other tags crucial for the image. When tagging photos of people, include specific details like gender, nationality, attire, actions, pose, expressions, accessories, makeup, composition type, age, etc. For other image categories, apply appropriate and common descriptive tags as well. Recognize and tag any celebrities, well-known landmark or IPs if clearly featured in the image. Your tags should be accurate, non-duplicative, and within a 20-75 word count range. These tags will use for image re-creation, so the closer the resemblance to the original image, the better the tag quality. Tags should be comma-separated. Exceptional tagging will be rewarded with $10 per image.

As an AI image tagging expert, please provide precise architecture terms as tags for this image to enhance CLIP model's understanding of the content. Choose succinct and comprehensive keywords or phrases, steering clear of elaborate sentences and extraneous conjunctions. Prioritize the tags by relevance. Your tags should capture key elements such as the main building type, background content, design style, building composition, material and color tone, image quality, filter, and camera specifications, and any other tags crucial for the image. When tagging images of buildings, follow this structured format: First, identify the main building type (e.g., residence, apartment, villa, office, skyscraper, museum, gallery, library, exhibition center, school, theater, CBD, hotel, mall, store, hospital, stadium, factory, airport, traffic station, etc.) and describe the contents of the image background. Second, specify the viewpoint of the image: 'front view' for a direct frontal perspective, 'bird's eye view' for a high, overhead perspective, or omit if the view is from an angle. Third, indicate the completeness and scale of the building in the image: 'close shot' for incomplete buildings, 'prospect' for distant, small-scale buildings, or omit for full, normal-sized buildings. Four, detail the architectural style of the building using at least four professional architectural terms (e.g., 'modern style', 'vernacular', 'stacking boxes').Five, highlight specific parts or details of the main subject (e.g., facade, roof, stairs, entrance) and describe their colors and materials, using multiple terms if necessary. Six, if the image features special lighting effects or a cinematic lighting setup, use 'movie light scene'. If the image is taken at night and has a relatively dark color tone, add 'night scene'. Generated tags should be accurate, non-duplicative, and within a 30-75 words count range. These tags will use for image re-creation, so the closer the resemblance to the original image, the better the tag quality. Tags should be comma-separated. Exceptional tagging will be rewarded with $20 per image.
